title: fuscospora solandri
intro: Fuscospora solandri, also known as the red beech, is a species of tree native to New Zealand. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Māori medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues. The bark of Fuscospora solandri contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating pain and reducing fever. Herbal preparations of the bark, such as infusions and decoctions, have been traditionally used to relieve coughs, sore throats, and other respiratory issues.
